message:
Add tests and enhancements to content indexing subsystem

Implement test coverage for Tantivy schema validation and document construction;
expand extractor interface with telemetry fields.

## content-index/src/lib.rs (§5.2: Content index schema)

### Test: schema_has_expected_fields
Validates ContentFields struct contains all required Tantivy field handles:
- doc_key, volume, name, path, ext, size, modified (metadata)
- content_lang, content (full-text fields)
- Verifies schema.get_field_entry() returns named fields (not panics)

Purpose:
- Catches schema definition regressions (e.g., typo in field name)
- Documents expected field set for future maintainers

### Test: to_document_sets_key_and_content
Verifies `to_document()` correctly populates Tantivy Document:
- Creates ContentDoc with key = DocKey::from_parts(1, 2)
- Converts to tantivy::Document via to_document(&doc, &fields)
- Asserts doc_key field contains packed u64 value
- Validates Value::U64 variant (correct field type)

Purpose:
- Ensures DocKey packing preserves volume+file ID through serialization
- Verifies content field is set (primary search target)

### Why these tests matter
- Tantivy schema changes can silently break queries if field names drift
- DocKey packing bugs would corrupt index lookups (§2.1)
- Tests serve as executable documentation of index structure

## content-index/Cargo.toml
Add test dependencies (implicitly):
- `tantivy` already in workspace deps (schema::Value import)
- No new deps required; tests use existing API

## content-extractor/src/lib.rs (§6: Extraction subsystem)

### ExtractorType enum
Categorizes extraction method used:
```rust
pub enum ExtractorType {
    Extractous,  // Primary: Apache Tika via Rust
    IFilter,     // Fallback: Windows COM IFilter
    Ocr,         // Last resort: Tesseract OCR
}
```

### ExtractedContent::extractor_used field
New field tracking which extractor succeeded:
```rust
pub struct ExtractedContent {
    pub key: DocKey,
    pub text: String,
    pub lang: Option<String>,
    pub truncated: bool,
    pub extractor_used: ExtractorType, // NEW
}
```

### Rationale
Telemetry and debugging:
- Metrics can report: "80% Extractous, 15% IFilter, 5% OCR"
- Performance analysis: Compare extraction latency by type
- Failure diagnosis: If OCR usage spikes, investigate upstream formats
- Config tuning: Disable slow extractors if success rate low

### Usage in worker
```rust
let content = extractor_stack.extract(key, &ctx)?;
metrics.record_extraction(content.extractor_used, elapsed);
if content.extractor_used == ExtractorType::Ocr {
    tracing::info!(doc_key=%key, "OCR extraction required");
}
```

### Impact on existing code
- `extract_placeholder()` updated to return Extractous by default
- Real extractors will set appropriate type on success
- Zero overhead: enum is Copy, no allocations

## content-extractor/Cargo.toml
No new dependencies; ExtractorType is pure enum

## Impact
- Content index has test safety net for schema evolution
- Extraction telemetry ready for operational insights
- Debugging improved: "Why is this file slow?" → check extractor_used
- Foundation for adaptive strategies (skip OCR if queue deep, etc.)

Next steps:
- Implement real Extractous integration (c00.5.2)
- Wire extractor_used into worker metrics (c00.5.4)
- Add schema migration tests (old index → new schema) (c00.4.5)
- Expand content-index tests: query execution, ranking (c00.5.5)

Related: PLAN §5.2 (content schema), §6 (extraction), §10.3 (metrics), AGENTS.md (tests)
